ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for steamed tofu with minced pork; closest defensible anchor is tofu-based food product (FOODON_03315595).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Tofu, cooked) used as closest defensible anchor; no composite steamed tofu with minced pork entry exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite dish; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 serving (approximately 1 block of tofu with minced pork topping)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is approximately 200 g, consisting of one block of steamed tofu topped with seasoned minced pork, as commonly plated in Singaporean home cooking and hawker-style meals.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed Tofu with Minced Pork — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Steamed Tofu with Minced Pork (cr_id F09050011). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and sugar values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (tofu + ground pork) where HPB does not report these fields directly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; tofu and soy-based foods reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for steamed tofu with minced pork. GI estimated at 15 based on plain tofu GI of approximately 15 (low GI, University of Sydney GI Database). The minced pork component contains negligible carbohydrate and does not materially alter the GI. Carbohydrate per serving (200 g × 3.79 g/100 g = 7.58 g); GL = (15 × 7.58) / 100 = 1.14, rounded to 1.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 274 mg per 100 g — borderline elevated for a home-cooked dish
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100–150 g and request reduced soy sauce in preparation to keep per-meal sodium contribution below 400 mg; the calcium (118 mg/100 g) and potassium (~185 mg/100 g) content provide partial vasodilatory offset but do not fully neutralise the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (3.79 g/100 g) and high protein (11.42 g/100 g) supporting postprandial glucose stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Steamed tofu with minced pork is well-suited for people with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate content (3.79 g/100 g), low GI (~15), and high protein (11.42 g/100 g) which slows gastric emptying and blunts postprandial glucose rise; avoid adding sweet sauces or sugar-based marinades that would increase the glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.2 g/100 g) from minced pork and borderline sodium (274 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, this dish is acceptable in moderate portions (100–150 g) as the soy isoflavones in tofu support LDL reduction; however, the saturated fat contribution from minced pork (~3.2 g/100 g) and borderline sodium (274 mg/100 g) warrant portion control and preference for leaner pork mince or substitution with minced chicken to reduce saturated fat intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Minced pork (red meat) contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6), amplifying pro-inflammatory pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the minced pork component, which is a red meat rich in arachidonic acid that amplifies inflammatory pathways; tofu alone is anti-inflammatory and RA-suitable, so replacing minced pork with minced chicken, turkey, or a tofu-only preparation is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Tofu with Minced Pork (蒸豆腐肉碎) is a classic Singaporean Chinese home-cooked and hawker dish consisting of a block of soft or firm tofu topped with seasoned minced pork and gently steamed until the pork is cooked through. The dish is typically seasoned with light soy sauce, sesame oil, and ginger, producing a savoury, umami-rich flavour with a silky, tender texture from the tofu and a slightly firmer bite from the pork. It is commonly served as part of a shared rice meal in Singaporean households and coffee shops. Nutritionally, the dish is protein-dense (11.42 g/100 g) from both the tofu and pork, moderate in fat (10.78 g/100 g), and very low in carbohydrate (3.79 g/100 g), making it suitable for low-carbohydrate and diabetic-friendly dietary patterns. Sodium is borderline at 274 mg/100 g, primarily from soy sauce seasoning, and warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension. Calcium content is notable at 118 mg/100 g, contributed by the tofu. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 15 (low), consistent with plain tofu. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following halal dietary requirements due to the pork content. It is a soft-textured dish appropriate for elderly individuals or those requiring easy-to-chew foods.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical entry (cr_id F09050011) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, sugar, potassium, and cholesterol estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (firm tofu + ground pork) and documented accordingly. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database tofu reference. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.2 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (4.1 g/100 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.5 g/100 g), sugar (0.8 g/100 g), potassium (185 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (28 mg/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (firm tofu + ground pork, approximately 60:40 ratio by weight) as HPB Foodo does not report these fields for this entry. 2. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this composite Singaporean dish are not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central at the composite dish level as of 2026. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible for steamed tofu and fresh pork mince with no hydrogenated fat ingredients. 4. The input dietary_tags included 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' which are incorrect for a dish containing minced pork; these tags have been removed. 5. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate based on plain tofu GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement exists for this composite dish. 6. Ontology codes are approximate; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 entry exists for this composite dish.
